heading: Provision of veterinary services
label: 1060c Provision of veterinary services

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: A veterinary professional described in subsection (b) may provide veterinary services in any State, the District of Columbia, or a territory or possession of the United States, without regard to where such veterinary professional or the patient animal are located, if the provision of such services is within the scope of the authorized duties of such veterinary professional for the Department of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: a member of the armed forces, a civilian employee of the Department of Defense, or otherwise credentialed and privileged at a Federal veterinary institution or location designated by the Secretary of Defense for purposes of this section; or

children.label: B
children.text: a member of the National Guard performing training or duty under ;section 502(f) of title 32
certified as a veterinary professional by a certification recognized by the Secretary of Defense; and
currently licensed by a State, the District of Columbia, or a territory or possession of the United States to provide veterinary services.
